finding: Reference-detection conventions (FRAME_KEYS/COLUMN_KEYS/COLUMN_LIST_KEYS) and per-root maintenance policies are hardcoded frozensets in meta_refs.py. The registry (meta_reference_surfaces) now mirrors them, but the code does not read the registry; consistency is enforced only by tests/architecture/semantic_invariants/test_meta_reference_surfaces.py.
notes: mirror-plus-guard is the deliberate Slice 1 design; generating the frozensets from the registry is a possible later step

finding: A new meta attribute holding a column name escapes rename/drop propagation silently unless it lies on an explicitly handled path of a supported root, or matches the FRAME_KEYS+COLUMN_KEYS/COLUMN_LIST_KEYS convention inside a scanned root. Supported roots are exempt from the scan, so unhandled paths inside them escape even when they use conventional key names.
notes: escapes produce no report entry; the operator cannot see them in a dry run

finding: _meta.helper_policies.lookup (written by configure_lookup_helpers, consumed by add_lookup_helpers) stores per-lookup column names (keys, allowed/default helpers, sort_by) under the supported root helper_policies, but _handle_helper_policies maintains only the fk subtree. Renaming or dropping a column referenced by a lookup policy silently dangles it - a live instance of GAP-META-REF-CONVENTION-ESCAPE.
notes: candidate fix: either maintain helper_policies.lookup like fk, or route unhandled helper_policies subtrees to the blocked-scan instead of exempting the whole root

finding: Table/key maintenance rows (TRANS-ADD-TABLE, TRANS-REMOVE-TABLE, TRANS-RENAME-TABLE, TRANS-RENAME-KEY) remain unimplemented candidates; the implemented schema maintenance is column-level only (TRANS-SCHEMA-COLUMN-MAINTENANCE). The column implementation does not discharge these rows.
notes: kept as candidates on purpose; promoting or retiring them is a semantic decision outside Slice 1
